title: Accessibility variants (left-handed/low-vision/ink-saving)
context: Accessibility is a first-class output of this project, not a toggle bolted on later. Three accessibility variants are required at M1: left-handed (direction arrows and start-dots adjusted so left-handed learners are not disadvantaged by guide placement), low-vision (increased contrast and line spacing for legibility), and ink-saving (reduced fill/line weight for inkjet frugality). Variants must compose with each other and with the four sheet templates. Output must be verified on real printed paper, not just on screen.
objective: Implement left-handed, low-vision, and ink-saving StyleVariant modes in the renderer; ensure variants compose correctly with all four sheet templates; verify legibility on real printed paper (grayscale-safe); obtain accessibility reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Left-handed variant adjusts guideline arrows, start-dots, and any direction cues so they do not disadvantage left-handed learners (e.g. start-dots and arrows positioned on the left side of each cell).
Low-vision variant increases line contrast and inter-line spacing to meet legibility standards; verified distinguishable in grayscale.
Ink-saving variant reduces line weight and fill density without losing formation clarity.
Variants compose: left-handed + low-vision and other combinations produce correct output without layout errors.
All variants compose with all four sheet templates (trace, fade, copy, free-practice).
Output verified legible on real printed paper in grayscale — not only on screen.
Accessibility reviewer signs off on the variant outputs.
Golden-file SVG tests for each variant; determinism preserved; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md section 6 (StyleVariant — left-handed, low-vision, ink-saving), section 9 M1
PLAN.md section 5 (Accessibility as first-class output, grayscale-safe)
PLAN.md Appendix A improvement 9 (accessibility first-class, not a toggle)
output: Left-handed, low-vision, and ink-saving StyleVariant implementations in packages/core, with golden-file SVG tests and accessibility reviewer sign-off — committed via PR with DCO sign-off.
